normalized_title: State Planning Policy 7.3 Residential Design Codes Volume 2 - Apartments
scope_summary: R-Codes Volume 2 provides performance-based planning and design standards for residential apartments (multiple dwellings) in high density coded areas (R80 and above and R-AC) across Western Australia, including dwellings within mixed use development in those areas. Part 2 sets default Primary Controls (building height, setbacks, plot ratio, building depth and separation) that local planning frameworks may vary; Parts 3 and 4 set element objectives with Acceptable Outcomes and design guidance for siting and designing apartment buildings. Unlike Volume 1, Acceptable Outcomes are not a deemed-to-comply pathway - proposals are assessed against the element objectives.

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: R80 and R100 coded sites (default, variable by local planning framework)
R80 coded sites (default; R100 1.3, R160 2.0, R-AC4 1.2, R-AC3 2.0, R-AC2 2.5, R-AC1 3.0)

key_numeric_standards.topic: minimum primary and secondary street setback
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: R80-R160 (2m or nil in centre/urban contexts; nil where commercial ground floor)
R80 and R100 coded sites (nil in some centre contexts)

key_numeric_standards.topic: average side setback where building length exceeds 16m

key_numeric_standards.applies_to: R80 coded sites (default; up to 4 storeys in R-AC1)

key_numeric_standards.topic: building separation - habitable rooms/balconies within site
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: buildings up to 4 storeys (18m for 5-8 storeys, 24m for 9+ storeys)

key_numeric_standards.topic: building separation - non-habitable rooms within site
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: buildings up to 4 storeys (6m for 5-8 storeys, 9m for 9+ storeys)
studio apartment (1 bed 47m2, 2 bed x 1 bath 67m2, 3 bed x 1 bath 90m2; +3m2 for second toilet, +5m2 for second bathroom)
apartments (other bedrooms 9m2; minimum internal dimension 3m excluding robes)
studio and 1 bed apartments (4m for other dwelling types)
all apartments, finished floor to finished ceiling (non-habitable rooms 2.4m)

key_numeric_standards.topic: single aspect open plan living area depth
key_numeric_standards.value: 3 x ceiling height (max 9 incl. 1.8 kitchen allowance)
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: single aspect open plan living areas
studio and 1 bedroom apartments, minimum dimension 2.0m (2 bed 10m2/2.4m; 3 bed 12m2/2.4m)

key_numeric_standards.topic: private open space - ground floor terrace
key_numeric_standards.applies_to: ground floor apartments or apartments with a terrace, minimum dimension 3m
studio dwellings, within or adjacent to private open space

cross_references.cited: State Planning Policy 7.0 Design of the Built Environment
Planning and Development (Local Planning Schemes) Regulations 2015
National Construction Code / Building Code of Australia

quality_flags: stylised/condensed display text garbled in places (e.g. Part 4 intro paragraph extracted as interleaved letter salad 'idpwc Gane Tn pe...'); body provisions are clean
multi-column Table 2.1 (primary controls) extracted with column headers separated from values, so attribution of some values to specific R-AC codings is inferred from layout order
mirrored-text artifacts from rotated figure labels (e.g. 'yradnuob tnorF')
Table 4.3b 'minimum internal dimension 13m' for bedrooms appears in extraction where the source likely reads '3m' (footnoted as 3m excluding robes elsewhere); verify against source PDF
